world_date: Month of the Ashen Blossom, Year 1 of the Hollow Accord

recent_events.text: War-Readers delivered their verdict: the raid was preordained in shadow-script. Elowen dispatches three Wayfarer war-bands into Faerwood's eastern fringe.
Prince Caeris Thorne vanished from the palace quietly. No announcement. His chambers were found empty, bed unslept, a single pressed moonflower left on the pillow.
Armed reformers breached the upper gate of the sealed district threshold. Ironmaul's guards held — but seventeen reformers now barricade the antechamber within.
A Gilgeth war-band and a Groth war-band collided at the Ashmark Ridge. Forty dead. Neither side found what killed the caravans. The war between them has begun anyway.
Admiral Electorate sent a formal letter to Aldric Thorne — not the King — proposing a 'mutual governance compact.' Aldric accepted within the hour. Roderic was not informed.

active_tensions.description: Wayfarer war-bands now active in Faerwood's eastern fringe. Clan Arkenor is preparing counter-ambush positions. First blood of open war within weeks.
Reformers hold the threshold antechamber. Ironmaul cannot flood or collapse it without killing Kessa — if she still lives. Standoff calcifies into siege.

active_tensions.factions: Twin Cities — Aldric vs Roderic vs Tidefall
active_tensions.description: Aldric's compact with Tidefall bypasses the King entirely. Roderic's performance of frailty may have become a trap he can no longer escape.
Open war erupts at Ashmark Ridge. The true killer of the caravans remains unknown. Gnomish glass-blades and their wielders have vanished back into the passes.

character_updates.name: Sovereign Elowen Silverleaf
character_updates.status: Has not slept in six days by her handmaiden's count. Reads the War-Reader scrolls each dawn. She is not preparing for war — she is preparing for something after it.

character_updates.name: Prince Aldric Thorne
character_updates.status: Signed the mutual governance compact with Tidefall. Now effectively co-governing Eldoria. His father has not yet responded publicly. That silence is either consent or coiling.

character_updates.name: Prince Caeris Thorne
character_updates.status: Missing. Palace rumor says he went north. A Gloomspire gnome courier was seen leaving the palace the same night Caeris disappeared.

character_updates.name: Thane Ulric Ironmaul
character_updates.status: Ordered the threshold antechamber sealed from above. Has not given the collapse order. His hesitation is noticed — and weaponized by both sides.

character_updates.name: Queen Lythara the Veiled
character_updates.status: Has summoned Clan Arkenor's commander to her private court. She emerged from the meeting alone. The commander has not been seen since.

character_updates.name: Kessa Stonebraid
character_updates.status: Confirmed alive — reformers in the antechamber report contact through a crack in the lower stone. She is speaking to something down there with her.

faction_morale.reason: Arkenor's commander disappearing after a private audience with Lythara has the clans uneasy. Nythariel openly questions who she will vanish next.
The threshold standoff has split even Ironmaul's loyalists. Soldiers refusing to follow collapse orders. Authority is dissolving in real time.
War-bands deployed with purpose and discipline. Grief has fully militarized. Wayfarers fight with eerie, silent precision — it unsettles even allies.
Aldric's compact and Caeris's disappearance in the same month has fractured the court irreparably. Merchants have begun relocating families, not just coin.
The compact with Aldric is their greatest political victory in a generation. Ships remain in harbor — now with tacit legal cover. Ambition validated.
First open battle at Ashmark Ridge. Both cities are bleeding. The real enemy moves unseen. War-bands too addicted and enraged to stop and think.
A Thorne prince on the move and a war to profit from. Gnomes are repositioning — not overextending. Glass contracts quietly doubled in two passes.
The novice speaking the unknown tongue has been brought before the Grand Druid. He has not been seen since. The standing stones pulse green nightly now.

whispers.text: Caeris Thorne went north with a gnome guide. He is heading for Frostvale — and he carries a sealed letter bearing the King's own signet. The real King's orders.
Kessa Stonebraid is not speaking to the darkness in the deep mines. She is listening. And she says it has a name.
The Frostwarden elders moved at last. One step south. In unison. After three weeks of stillness. Whatever they are watching, it moved first.
Lythara did not kill Arkenor's commander. She sent him somewhere. Clan Velorn say he was seen boarding a ship with no flag at a Faerwood coastal inlet.

weather_and_omens.condition: The second moon-shadow has resolved into a shape — vaguely antlered. Clan Nythariel druids say it is a Wayfarer war-omen. Clan Velorn say it is older than that.
The pulse in the deep stone has a rhythm now. Miners with ear to the wall count it: 43 beats, silence, 43 beats. It matches no known geological phenomenon.
After the Ashmark Ridge battle, crows gathered in numbers not seen in living memory. They did not feed on the dead. They sat, and watched, and left before dawn.
A tide rolled in against the wind in the harbor — against all sailing logic. Tidefall navigators on deck went pale and said nothing to their officers.
The novice has begun drawing maps. Not of Aeloria. The Grand Druid has locked the monastery scriptorium. Three scholars were turned away at the gate this week.

absorbed_lore: Elowen deployed Wayfarer war-bands into Faerwood's eastern fringe after War-Readers confirmed Lythara's name was preordained in shadow-script.
Arkenor's clan commander was summoned privately by Lythara and has not been seen since — possibly dispatched on a covert mission by sea.
Prince Caeris Thorne vanished from the palace; a Gloomspire gnome courier departed the same night, suggesting gnome-escorted northern passage.
Caeris reportedly carries a sealed letter with King Roderic's true signet — suggesting Roderic is conducting a shadow campaign behind his performed frailty.
Aldric Thorne signed a mutual governance compact with Tidefall Admiral Electorate, effectively co-governing Eldoria without the King's knowledge.
Reformers breached the threshold antechamber in Lostfeld; Ironmaul ordered it sealed above but has not given the collapse order — authority fracturing.
Kessa Stonebraid confirmed alive, in contact with reformers through a crack; she reports something in the deep with a name, and she is listening to it.
Gilgeth and Groth orc war-bands clashed at Ashmark Ridge — forty dead, open war begun; the true caravan killer remains unidentified.
The three Frostwarden Ice Dragon elders took one step south in unison after three weeks of stillness — a significant omen.
The druid novice at Stonebreak is now drawing maps of an unknown place; the Grand Druid has locked the scriptorium and turned away scholars.
